figures.caption: Fig. 1: Architecture of the Proposed Transpilation Framework. (a) User OpenQASM 3.0 programs are parsed by the pyqasm frontend, transpiled into QIR-compliant CUDA-Q kernels, and executed by CUDA-Q. (b) QASM is lowered from AST to MLIR (classical-control + Quake dialects), then translated to LLVM IR/QIR and emitted as executable kernels. (c) CUDA-Q executes on qpp (CPU state-vector), cuQuantum (GPU state-vector), and Stim (stabilizer sampling) backends.
